Empires & Puzzles Costumed Senan Review: Tentacles, Trauma, and Top-Tier Tiles

If the original Senan was a dusty old book, Costumed Senan is the Eldritch horror that wrote it. With a massive stat overhaul, a terrifying new passive, and double the speed ramp-up, this Slayer has gone from 'benchwarmer' to 'nightmare fuel'. Is he worth the summon? Absolutely.
Costumed Senan: The Cephaloid Servant Rises
Costumed Senan: Tentacles, Trauma, and Top-Tier Tiles

Costumed Senan, released in April 2025, is a spectacular reinvention of the classic Slayer hero. By trading his spectral book for some very questionable tentacle appendages (and a Wizard class), he has transformed from a sluggish relic into a modern powerhouse. He retains the core "Slayer" identity—ramping up speed and healing—but does it so much faster and harder that he feels like a completely different hero.

of Senan. If you are looking for the review of his base version, check our separate guide (spoiler: it's depressing).

The "Cephaloid" theme is a bold departure from the original. Gone is the weary scholar; in his place is a cultist fully consumed by the depths. The teal and purple color palette is striking, and the tentacles add a dynamic, creepy flow to the card. It clearly signals: "I am not just cursing you; I am dragging you under." It’s deliciously eerie and fits the upgraded power level perfectly.
A leap of 400 points over his base form.
These are competitive modern stats. He can take a hit, and unlike his base form, his "Hit All" special actually hurts.
With over 2300 HP, he is a tanky beast. This durability is crucial because it guarantees he lives long enough for his Slayer stacks to kick in.
Special Skill: Curse of the Cephaloid
"Curse of the Cephaloid" is a masterclass in AoE pressure.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Slayer family bonus (50/75/100% chance for stacks) is now even more valuable because the stacks themselves (on the costume) are twice as potent. If you pair him with another Slayer, that passive mana gen tick can accidentally push him to full mana when you least expect it.
This is huge. 544 damage to all enemies is basically a second special skill. It ensures that even if the initial hit doesn't kill, the bleed often finishes them off.

is a top-tier Aether Power. Unlike the base version's "Fiend Resist," Damage Reduction ensures Costumed Senan stays alive to keep stacking his mana. It synergizes perfectly with his high HP and self-healing stacks.
Base Senan was forgotten. Costumed Senan arrived in 2025 and immediately demanded respect.
He has successfully power-crept himself. His stats and mechanics are fully aligned with the 2025/2026 meta.
He is a staple in Rush Wars, a monster in Towers/Events, and a very strong contender for standard raid defense tanks or flanks.
The Costume is superior in every conceivable way.
Base Senan is a tortoise. Costumed Senan is a tortoise that straps a rocket to its back after turn 3.
Costume deals significantly more upfront damage + massive Bleed damage.
Costume has nearly +1000 HP (with LB2 stats) over the original.

Stronger fiends make it very hard for enemies to recover.
His Bleed and Fiends can be cleansed.

He hits all, but taunt heroes can absorb the brunt of the special (though the fiends still land on everyone? No, usually blocked by taunt if it prevents targeting).
He is S-Tier here. The slow start is negated, and he just stacks speed for fun.
In long fights, he reaches 10 stacks quickly and becomes a machine gun.
A nightmare tank or flank. If you don't kill him fast, he becomes unstoppable.

If you are lucky enough to pull this costume, you have a top-tier hero. Prioritize leveling him immediately. He will carry you through almost all content.

Costumed Senan is a worthy investment for Alpha Aethers. Unlike his base form, he has the stats and mechanics to compete in the top 100. He is a fantastic answer to passive-heavy teams because his Bleed and Fiends provide constant pressure that bypasses many defenses.
"That double permanent +12% mana gen is his secret super weapon here. The guaranteed pretty high bleed damage passive is also nasty." -
A sharp observer noting the real power lies in the passive.
"I would rather compare Costumed Senan to these heroes... Costumed Senan has better stats, but worse damage." -
A player comparing him to heavy hitters, missing the point of his ramp-up speed.
"Is Senan now legit? The passives on this costume are solid and that mana bump and heal are not to be scoffed at."

Costumed Senan is the hero Senan was always meant to be. He retains the unique "Slayer" identity but sheds the sluggishness that held him back. With tentacles flailing and bleed damage ticking, he is a terrifying presence on the battlefield.
